chunks.title: Catalog Description and Special Course Designation
chunks.content:
Computer Supported Collaborative Work. (3-0). Credit 3. Covers design, implementation and use of technical systems that support people working cooperatively; draws from the research area of Computer Supported Cooperative Work (CSCW) and includes current theoretical, practical, technical and social issues in CSCW and future directions of the field. Prerequisite: CSCE 671 or CSCE 610 or approval of instructor.

Special Course Designation
None

chunks.title: Course Prerequisites
chunks.content: Prerequisite/Corequisite(s): CSCE 671 or CSCE 610 or approval of instructor.

chunks.title: Course Learning Outcomes
chunks.content:
Upon completion of this course, the learner will be able to:
• describe the history of asynchronous and synchronous collaborative software environments
• prioritize issues and identify trade-offs in the design of collaborative software
• design software capabilities that facilitate awareness, communication and coordination
• analyze use contexts in terms of social processes, time and place settings, and available resources
• assess the need for reciprocity, critical mass, and exception handling for collaborative applications
• design, implement and evaluate software to support collaboration in specific settings

chunks.title: Textbook and/or Resource Materials
chunks.content:
This material Is: Required
online papers from conferences and journals

chunks.title: Grading Policy
chunks.content:
Assignment 1: 10%
Team project: 35%
Readings write-ups: 35%
Readings presentations: 10%
Attendance (via sign-in sheet): 10%
Grades will be on a 10 point scale: 90-100 = A, 80-89 = B, 70-79 = C, 60-69 = D, and <60 = F.

chunks.title: Late Work Policy and Course Specific Late Work Policy
chunks.content:
Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy (Student Rule 7).

Write-ups late policy:
50% is immediately deducted from your grade if turned in late. 5% more is deducted for every day late up to a maximum of one week after the original due date. For example, if a write up is turned in between 48-72 hours late, the highest grade it could receive is 40%. Do the reading and turn in the write-ups before class.
Other assignments late policy:
10% is deducted per day late up to a maximum of one week after the original due date.

chunks.title: Course Schedule and Significant Due Dates
chunks.content:
week 1: introductions, CSCW history and central issues
week 2: designing for artifact and human awareness
week 3: forms of communication
week 4: content sharing systems and issues
week 5: collaborative decision making
week 6: voting models and balkanization
week 7: meeting capture and reuse
week 8: crowdsourcing and its issues
week 9: online communities and virtual teams
week 10: shared user history and its visualization
week 11: collaborative search and design
week 12: software supporting social skill development
week 13: collaboration in online games
week 14: supporting software teams
week 15: student project presentations

Significant Due Dates:
Assignment 1 due January 28
Final project presentations due April 27-28
Final project reports due May 1
student presentations will occur throughout the semester a sign up sheet will be used to determine which student(s) present which readings/days
smaller project activities will be due once per week and readings write-ups will be due on average twice per week
